title: STARTPREFIXMAPPING
text: STARTPREFIXMAPPING Operation activated by the SAX parser when it encounters a start tag with one or more xmlns (namespace) specifications. Explain this code operation STARTPREFIXMAPPING params string Prefix: in string URI: in endparams Implement your code here end; STARTPREFIXMAPPING Copy Parameters Prefix —namespace identifier. URI —namespace identified by the prefix. Description When this operation is defined, it is called by PARSE and PARSEFILE when xmlns is encountered inside a start tag. For example, given the following start tag: Explain this code <a:y xmlns="http://www.mywebsite.com/default"> Copy STARTPREFIXMAPPING is invoked three times, with the following values: Table 1. Invocation Parameter Values For XMl Value of Prefix Value of URI Comments xmlns="http://www.mywebsite.com/default" "" "http://www.mywebsite.com/default" An xmlns specification that is not followed by a colon and a prefix name sets a default namespace (that is, the URI) for all tags up to the closing end tag. Any tag, up to the closing end tag, that doesn’t have a prefix is considered to belong to the namespace indicated by this URI. xmlns:a="http://www.mywebsite.com/a.html" "a" "http://www.mywebsite.com/a.html" Any tag, up to the closing end tag, that starts with a: is considered to belong to the namespace indicated by this URI ( "http://www.mywebsite.com/a.html" ). xmlns:a="http://www.mywebsite.com/b.html" "b" "http://www.mywebsite.com/b.html" Any tag, up to the closing end tag, that starts with b: is considered to belong to the namespace indicated by this URI ( "http://www.mywebsite.com/b.html" ). Related Links PARSE Parses a specified XML stream. PARSEFILE Parses an XML document in the specified file. ENDPREFIXMAPPING Operation activated by the SAX parser for each namespace specification in a start tag, when the parser encounters the corresponding end tag. Was this topic helpful? Like Dislike
